title: rubus procerus f. obtusangulus
intro: Rubus procerus f. obtusangulus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of bramble native to North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves, which contain a compound called salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin. The thimbleberry is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the thimbleberry plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations and alleviate pain.
